feat: enforce beta generation quota across backend and UI

- increment profiles.generations_used after successful done events with non-blocking error logging

- add websocket handler tests for quota exhaustion, successful increments, and no increment on pipeline failure
- update generation pipeline test setup to stub quota checks and avoid external profile access

- load profile quota from Supabase on app load and refresh quota after successful generation
- show remaining generation quota in the top bar and on the questionnaire generate screen
- disable Generate Architecture at zero remaining and show beta exhaustion messaging
- stop generating state when websocket error responses arrive

Closes #12

fix(cli): restore and harden session/settings migration (#27)

* fix(settings): support nested env/defaults structure and new env var names

- Rename settings file from setting.json to settings.json (with legacy fallback)
- Support nested structure with 'env' and 'defaults' sections
- Add support for AGENTLET_API_KEY and AGENTLET_BASE_URL env vars
- Maintain backward compatibility with flat structure and OPENAI_* env vars
- Add comprehensive tests for new functionality
- Extract shared helpers for validation and settings construction
- Add change detection for os.environ mutations

Fixes settings loading to work with user's ~/.agentlet/settings.json format.

* test: add explicit monkeypatch cleanup for env vars in settings tests

Add monkeypatch.delenv() calls before load_settings() to ensure proper
cleanup of environment variables set during tests. This makes the test
isolation explicit and prevents potential pollution of the global env state.

* refactor(settings): remove env section support from settings.json

- Remove _apply_env_section() function that loaded env vars from settings
- Simplify _load_nested_settings() to only handle defaults section
- Update load_settings() to detect nested structure by 'defaults' key only
- Remove tests for env section functionality
- Remove non-existent inject_project_env import from test script

BREAKING CHANGE: The 'env' section in settings.json is no longer supported.
Environment variables should be set externally (e.g., in shell profile).

* refactor(settings): clarify docstring for load_settings

Remove the confusing mention of 'env' section no longer being
supported since this feature was never implemented.

* feat(settings): add LiteLLM-standard provider-specific env var resolution

- Add provider-to-env mappings for API keys and base URLs following
  LiteLLM naming conventions (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, AZURE_API_KEY, etc.)
- Unify _resolve_api_key and _resolve_api_base into single
  _resolve_provider_env function with keyword parameters
- Normalize provider name once to avoid redundant .lower() calls
- Add comprehensive tests for provider-specific env var resolution
- Maintain backward compatibility with OPENAI_API_KEY as fallback

Priority order:
1. AGENTLET_API_KEY (project-specific override)
2. {PROVIDER}_API_KEY (LiteLLM standard)
3. OPENAI_API_KEY (backward compatibility)
4. settings.json value

* chore: add AI coding agent directories to .gitignore

* refactor(sessions): migrate session storage to ~/.agentlet

Migrate session and history storage from {cwd}/.agentlet/ to ~/.agentlet/
to make agentlet behave more like a standard Python tool.

Changes:
- Sessions stored at ~/.agentlet/sessions/{cwd_hash}/
- History stored globally at ~/.agentlet/history
- Added get_data_dir() and _cwd_hash() helper functions
- Updated SessionStore to accept optional data_dir parameter
- Added pytest fixture to reduce test boilerplate
- Updated documentation for new storage paths

The cwd_hash is the first 16 chars of MD5 hash of the resolved cwd path,
providing isolation between projects while keeping data centralized.

- Complete rewrite of README with quick start, configuration, and CLI usage
- New docs/cli-usage.md with detailed guide covering:

* fix(cli): harden session hashing and migration

Summary:
- replace MD5 cwd hashing with truncated SHA-256 session bucket hashes
- bound cross-bucket session lookup and warn on duplicate session ids
- make legacy migration writes atomic and type CLI setting overrides

Rationale:
- avoid weak hashing primitives in filesystem path generation
- keep session resolution deterministic when the same id appears twice
- prevent concurrent migration writers from overwriting each other

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: Bab MCP server — initial release

Bab is an MCP server that delegates AI coding tasks to CLI agents
(Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, OpenCode) via a plugin system,
powered by Vercel AI SDK across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and OpenRouter.

Core features:
- MCP server with 17 tools: delegate, consensus, chat, analyze, debug,
  codereview, refactor, testgen, docgen, secaudit, thinkdeep, tracer,
  planner, precommit, challenge, list_models, version
- Plugin system: manifest-driven, adapter-based, dynamic import with
  realpath containment, Zod-validated manifests, atomic install/remove/list
  with staging and rollback
- ModelGateway: unified AI SDK routing across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google,
  OpenRouter, and plugin-hosted models (pluginId/modelName)
- Consensus tool: multi-model routing, parallel execution, per-model
  temperature and thinking_mode
- Agent Skills generation: auto-discovers host agents, writes SKILL.md
  with tool catalog and plugin references
- Selfupdate: fail-secure with SHA256 checksum verification, origin
  validation, and atomic binary replacement with rollback
- Install script: platform detection, checksum verification, brew fallback,
  sudo escalation messaging
- SDK + test-utils exported for plugin authors

Security:
- Copilot --allow-all is an opt-in via role args
- Plugin install trust warning + SECURITY.md with disclosure policy
- Array-based spawn (no shell injection), realpath containment
- Env stripping: PATH, LD_PRELOAD, DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES, NODE_OPTIONS,
  BAB_INTERNAL_* blocked
- YAML billion-laughs protection (maxAliasCount: 10)
- Timeout validation, GHA injection prevention

Tests:
- 211 unit tests + 8 integration tests (hermetic harness, isolated HOME,
  minimal ENV_PASSTHROUGH)
- E2E test for OpenCode plugin with stub binary
- CI: unit and e2e steps run separately

Co-authored-by: claude-opus-4-5 <claude-opus-4-5@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: buffer non-streaming response body for token reconciliation (closes #12) (#20)

* fix: buffer non-streaming response body for token reconciliation (closes #12)

Without a body_filter phase for non-streaming responses, cost_extractor
was never called and pessimistic TPM/TPD reservations were never refunded,
causing token budgets to drain faster than actual usage.

Changes:
- streaming.lua: import cost_extractor; add _reconcile_non_streaming() that
  buffers up to 1 MiB of response body per spec, extracts usage.total_tokens,
  and calls llm_limiter.reconcile() to refund unused tokens
- streaming.lua: body_filter() now handles the non-streaming path (active=false
  but key present) by buffering chunks and reconciling on EOF
- streaming.lua: init_stream() initialises body_buffer field
- streaming_spec.lua: install mock_cjson_safe; add 4 new scenarios covering
  full-body extraction, chunked delivery, missing usage field (no over-refund),
  and no-context passthrough

* test(e2e): add token reconciliation e2e test suite (closes #12)

Add mock LLM backend (nginx serving fixed usage JSON), a new edge service
in reverse_proxy mode with token_bucket_llm policy, and three e2e scenarios:

- pass-through: response body contains usage.total_tokens=50
- metric: fairvisor_token_reservation_unused_total emitted after reconcile
- refund: 5 consecutive requests succeed (reconciled ~50 tokens each vs
  pessimistic 1000, so budget is not exhausted)

Test runs as part of the nightly full e2e suite (pytest tests/e2e).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: two pre-existing bugs that prevented token_bucket_llm from working e2e

1. bundle_loader: validate_config was called on a shallow copy of
   algorithm_config, so computed fields (_tpm_bucket_config, default
   defaults etc.) were never written back to rule.algorithm_config.
   After validation, merge all non-algorithm keys back so request-time
   code sees the normalised config.

2. rule_engine: the final "allow" decision built at line 882 omitted
   rule_name, and limit_result.key was never set for token_bucket_llm.
   decision_api.lua:933 fell through to the fallback which concatenated
   nil rule_name, causing a 500.  Fix: track last_allow_rule_name, set
   limit_result.key = counter_key for allowed LLM checks, and include
   rule_name in the final allow decision.

These bugs were latent and exposed by the new e2e test suite added for
issue #12 / Feature 015.  All 3 e2e reconciliation tests now pass.

  developer-specific Linear project slug in git

* fix(symphony): address bugbot and format issues

Pass the PR head SHA into the land watcher review loop so human feedback checks do not fail at runtime. Reuse the shared review pagination helper and remove the committed personal workflow path from the Symphony setup docs.

Also apply the package-local Prettier formatting that the plugin CI check expects for the Playwright helper scripts.

* fix(symphony): preserve compose keys after port filtering

Share the compose YAML service-key matching used by the worktree runtime scripts so scalar-valued keys like restart, working_dir, and image correctly terminate a ports block. Add a regression test covering the dropped-key case and keep the assistant offset script aligned with the same behavior.

* fix(symphony): make compose helpers cwd-independent

Pass the repo path into the inline Python helpers so they can import shared compose parsing logic even when the shell script is launched from another working directory. Rename the shared regex to reflect that it intentionally marks the end of a ports block in both runtime scripts.

* fix(sdk-traffic): fall back to system python3 for one-shot assertions

When the python emitter is disabled via SIGIL_TRAFFIC_EMITTERS, setup_python
is skipped and the venv at PYTHON_VENV is never created. run_one_shot_assertions
unconditionally used the venv python binary, causing a runtime error.

The assertion script (assert-one-shot.py) only uses stdlib modules, so it
does not need the venv. Fall back to system python3 when the venv binary
does not exist.
